'''Adamastor''' was a largely [[Waterworld/Legends|aquatic world]]<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> situated in the [[Adamastor system/Legends|Adamastor system]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA Adamastor" /> Its surface featured a number of [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]].<ref name="The Floating Hospital" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
By approximately [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="3643 BBY">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "The Floating Hospital" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the game, the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> a group of [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]]<ref name="The Floating Hospital" />—a [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|hailing]] from<ref name="Manaan">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040205manaan1|series=Manaan: Depths of History|text=Part 1: Backwater}}</ref> the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]]<ref name="Manaan" />—operated a [[Adamastor medical facility|medical facility]] that floated on Adamastor's [[Water/Legends|oceans]]. At one point, they decided to retire the facility and abandon it to the planet's waves, with an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] subsequently sending a companion to retrieve anything of value from it. The companion went on to recover several experimental medical devices.<ref name="The Floating Hospital" />
{{Endgame}}

The [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Tolum Harous]] hailed from Adamastor, harboring an ever-lasting love for oceans. He went on to hold several honorary degrees from technological institutes throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and worked as an [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] for [[Hydrospeare Corporation]] beginning nearly [[28 BBY/Legends|thirty years]] prior to a point<ref name="AIR" /> in approximately [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].<ref name="2 ABY">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Adamastor was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Adamastor [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square L-9.<ref name="TEA Adamastor" /> Adamastor was mentioned in a Scavenging Crew Skill mission of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', a [[2011]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] developed by [[BioWare]], though the information regarding the mission's outcome was later removed from the game.<ref name="The Floating Hospital" />
